name: teams
context: Understand Linear team structure, workflow states, and how teams scope issues and cycles
info: Teams are the primary organizational unit in Linear. Every issue belongs to exactly one team. Teams define their own workflow states (the lifecycle stages an issue moves through), cycle cadence, default labels, and triage settings. Understanding teams is essential for any agent that creates or queries issues, because the teamId is a required field for issue creation and workflow states are team-specific. The GraphQL API provides queries for listing teams, their members, workflow states, labels, and cycles. Agents use this context to discover available teams before creating issues and to understand valid workflow state transitions.

sections.title: Listing teams
sections.body: Use the `teams` query to list all teams in the workspace. Each team has `id` (UUID), `name`, `key` (short prefix like ENG), `description`, and `members`. The `key` is used in issue identifiers — `ENG-42` means issue 42 in the ENG team. Filter teams with `teams(filter: { key: { eq: "ENG" } })`.

sections.title: Workflow states
sections.body: Each team defines its own workflow states with types: `backlog`, `unstarted`, `started`, `completed`, `cancelled`. States determine valid issue transitions. Query via `team(id).states`. When creating issues, you can set `stateId` to place the issue in a specific state. Default is typically the first `backlog` or `unstarted` state.

sections.title: Cycles and sprints
sections.body: Cycles are time-boxed sprints scoped to a team. Query `team(id).activeCycle` for the current sprint and `team(id).cycles` for history. Cycles have `startsAt`, `endsAt`, `number`, and issue associations. Teams configure cycle duration and auto-start in settings.

sections.title: Priority levels
sections.body: Issues have a priority field from 0 (none) to 4 (low). Counter-intuitively, 1 is urgent and 4 is low. Priority 0 means no priority set. Use `priority` field in issue queries and mutations. Priority is an integer, not a string.

links.context: Issues belong to teams — teamId is required for creation
Creating issues requires a valid teamId

author: dojo-community
